claim: The strait is a strategic asset; I will keep it closed and mined to inflict maximum economic pain on the US and its allies, even if it devastates Iran's own economy. I view any tanker transit as a violation of sovereignty and will order IRGC to fire on any vessel attempting passage.
beliefs: Closure forces US to negotiate
Mines are effective deterrent
Tanker operators will not risk attack
The strait is a strategic asset;
I will keep it closed and mined to inflict maximum economic pain on the US and its allies, even if it devastates Iran's own economy.
I view any tanker transit as a violation of sovereignty and will order IRGC to fire on any vessel attempting passage.
Domestic hardliners demand continued closure;
IRGC Quds Force advocates escalation
commitments: Keep strait closed until sanctions lifted
pressures: Domestic hardliners demand continued closure; IRGC Quds Force advocates escalation
supporting_evidence_ids: Iran shut the strait and reportedly mined parts of the waterway
historical_case_refs: Iran-Iraq War tanker war (1984-1988) where Iran mined strait and attacked shipping
distinguishing_observations: No tankers transit; IRGC boats active near mines; diplomatic channels silent
action_if_state: Order IRGC to maintain minefields, deploy speedboats to harass any tanker approaching strait, and publicly threaten to sink vessels that attempt transit without permission.
assumptions: US will not escalate to direct strikes on Iranian soil
latent_beliefs: Closure forces US to negotiate
Mines are effective deterrent
Tanker operators will not risk attack
The strait is a strategic asset;
I will keep it closed and mined to inflict maximum economic pain on the US and its allies, even if it devastates Iran's own economy.

Domestic hardliners demand continued closure;
IRGC Quds Force advocates escalation
known_commitments: Keep strait closed until sanctions lifted

claim: I can selectively allow tanker transits to signal flexibility or to reward allies, while keeping the strait nominally closed. I will permit a few Iranian-flagged tankers to pass to test US blockade, but will not allow 50 per day.
beliefs: Partial opening can split US coalition
Tanker operators will follow signals

historical_case_refs: Iran's 'oil for goods' swaps during sanctions (2012-2015)
distinguishing_observations: Occasional tanker transits under IRGC escort; mixed signals from Tehran

claim: My orders are to enforce a total blockade of Iranian ports and prevent any oil tanker from transiting the strait. I will board, divert, or disable any vessel attempting to carry Iranian oil, and I will not allow any tanker to pass until Iran agrees to demilitarize the strait.
beliefs: Blockade is legal under self-defense
Tanker operators will comply if enforcement is credible
Mines are a secondary threat
My orders are to enforce a total blockade of Iranian ports and prevent any oil tanker from transiting the strait.
I will board, divert, or disable any vessel attempting to carry Iranian oil, and I will not allow any tanker to pass until Iran agrees to demilitarize the strait.
Pentagon demands results;
allies in region want stability;
risk of escalation with Iran

Use minimum force but escalate if needed
pressures: Pentagon demands results; allies in region want stability; risk of escalation with Iran

historical_case_refs: US blockade of Iraq (1990-2003) - maritime interception operations
distinguishing_observations: US Navy vessels stop and inspect all vessels near strait; no tankers pass

assumptions: Iran will not attack US warships directly

claim: I have discretion to allow non-Iranian tankers to transit if they prove they are not carrying Iranian oil. I will focus on interdicting Iranian-flagged vessels and those suspected of sanctions evasion, but will let others pass to avoid international backlash.
beliefs: Total blockade is unsustainable politically
Risk of collision or incident is high
Tanker operators will cooperate if rules are clear
I have discretion to allow non-Iranian tankers to transit if they prove they are not carrying Iranian oil.
I will focus on interdicting Iranian-flagged vessels and those suspected of sanctions evasion, but will let others pass to avoid international backlash.
State Department wants to avoid alienating allies;
CENTCOM worries about force protection
pressures: State Department wants to avoid alienating allies; CENTCOM worries about force protection
supporting_evidence_ids: only about 7 ships transited the strait in the prior 24 hours, none of which were oil tankers carrying cargo for the global market
historical_case_refs: US maritime interception operations in Persian Gulf (1987-1988) - tanker reflagging
distinguishing_observations: Some tankers pass after inspection; US Navy sets up a 'safe corridor' for non-Iranian vessels
action_if_state: Establish a transit corridor for tankers that submit to inspection and prove non-Iranian cargo; allow up to 50 per day if they meet criteria.
assumptions: Iran will not attack transiting tankers
latent_beliefs: Total blockade is unsustainable politically

claim: The strait is too dangerous due to mines and military confrontation. We will not send any tankers through until there is a clear, internationally guaranteed safe passage agreement. The risk of being sunk or seized outweighs any profit.
beliefs: Mines are unpredictable
Insurance will not cover war risk
Crews refuse to sail

claim: We see a window to transit if we can secure insurance, pay for naval escort, or disguise cargo. Some operators will risk it for high profits, especially if US enforcement is selective. We will send tankers through if we believe we can pass undetected or with permission.
beliefs: US blockade is porous
Iran may allow passage for a fee
First movers will earn premium
